title: Pseudopanax macintyrei
intro: Pseudopanax macintyrei, also known as Macintyre's lacewood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine to treat various health issues, and its bioactive constituents, including lignans and flavonoids, are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These constituents have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although modern research is still in its early stages to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
